description: ORDERED, canonical deck-intake question file for the Presentations department. Mirrors the proven branding-questions.json schema. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH the runtime intake driver (scripts/deck-intake-driver.py) steps through ONE QUESTION AT A TIME. The agent does NOT decide the next question — the driver returns it from this file (lowest unanswered required id), so the 30-minute wandering interview that skips questions and re-asks answered ones is structurally impossible. SOP 9.0/9.1/9.2 (brainstorming-buddy-presentations-sops.md) is the doctrinal source; this file encodes the mode CHOICE first, then the six mandatory fields IN ORDER, then scope, then a session budget. The client experiences ONE seamless voice: the prompts here carry no routing/relay chatter ('relayed to the department', 'the Director noted', 'you'll see their next question shortly' are forbidden).

modeChoiceFirst.prompt: Before we dive in, how thorough do you want this? I can do it two ways. The QUICK way: I ask about 5 to 7 key questions and we lock it in fast. Or the IN-DEPTH way: we go back and forth on 10 to 20 questions and really flesh it out. Which do you want?
modeChoiceFirst.help: Quick is great when the idea is clear or the stakes are low. In-depth is best for a flagship talk or an idea that is still taking shape.
modeChoiceFirst.note: ALWAYS the first turn (it is not counted as one of the mandatory questions). Maps quick -> interview_mode:simple (SOP 9.1, <=7 questions), in-depth -> interview_mode:extensive (SOP 9.2, 10-20). The chosen value sizes the session budget below.

questions.prompt: In one or two sentences, what is this presentation about, and what is the single most important thing the audience should do or believe by the end?
questions.help: The one outcome anchors every later choice — the arc, the hook, the pitch. Keep it to the ONE thing that matters most.
questions.interviewGuidance: Scope-setter. Drill once if the answer is two competing goals — a deck can only land one #1 outcome.

questions.prompt: Will this be delivered by one person presenting live (a speaker on stage or on a call), or is it a general/standalone deck someone reads on their own?
questions.help: One-person decks are paced to a speaker and a talk track; general decks must stand alone without narration.
questions.interviewGuidance: Mandatory field 1/6. Gate AF-RENDERER/_chk_intake requires presentation_mode in {one-person, general}.

questions.prompt: About how long is the talk — roughly 20, 30, or 60 minutes (or tell me the exact number)?
questions.help: This sets the pacing floor (about 1.3 slides per minute) so the deck is paced, not a reading session.
questions.interviewGuidance: Mandatory field 2/6. Feeds _chk_intake (target_talk_minutes) + the AF-SLIDE-COUNT-FLOOR pacing floor.

questions.prompt: Do you have an exact number of slides you want? If you say 25, the deck will have exactly 25 — I never round it up or cut it down. If you'd rather I pick the right count for the length, just say 'you decide'.
questions.help: Your number is honored verbatim (25 -> 25, 50 -> 50, 500 -> 500). 'You decide' lets the pacing floor choose.
questions.interviewGuidance: Mandatory field 3/6. When the client states a number, record client_requested_slide_count EXACTLY (AF-SLIDE-COUNT-EXACT honors it verbatim; never floor up, cap down, default, or substitute, and never ask them to accept a different number). 'You decide' leaves it null so the pacing floor governs.

questions.prompt: Does this presentation end with an offer or pitch — a price, package, or call to buy — or is it a teaching and content-only presentation?
questions.help: Either is first-class. A teaching-only deck is never forced to have a pitch; a pitch deck gets the full offer arc.
questions.interviewGuidance: Mandatory field 4/6. Record an EXPLICIT boolean pitch_included (never default, never force a pitch). Gate AF-PITCH-FLAG-UNSET. Ask verbatim, once, early.

questions.prompt: Is there anything you already have that could help me build this — photos, a logo, brand colors, a rough or old deck, slides, or concepts? Drop them here.
questions.help: Anything you share gets fed into the build. If you have nothing, that's fine — just say so.
questions.interviewGuidance: Mandatory field 5/6. Set asset_intake_question_asked:true the moment it is asked (gate AF-ASSET-QUESTION-MISSING). If items are provided set assets_provided:true and capture each (an uploaded old deck is a scratch_deck); else assets_provided:false. Ask verbatim, once, early.

questions.prompt: For the look of your slides, do you have a particular image style in mind? You can: (1) point me at an existing deck, past designs, or reference images you want me to match; (2) tell me a saved style name from your library if you already have one; or (3) let me creatively develop a signature style for you. Which one?
questions.help: This keeps the deck from inventing a random look — it either matches your references, reuses a saved style, or develops one with you.
questions.interviewGuidance: Mandatory field 6/6. Set STYLE_SOURCE; a deck reaching Phase 2 with style_source unset would invent a look with no client direction (the cookie-cutter-typography defect). Ask verbatim, once, early (SOP-IMG-03 section 2).

questions.prompt: Who is in the room — who is this audience, and what do they care about most right now?
questions.help: The audience's current priority stack is what the deck re-ranks. Be specific about who they are and what is top of mind.
questions.interviewGuidance: Scope field. Extensive (in-depth) mode asks this; quick mode may skip if the topic answer already named the audience.

questions.prompt: When this presentation lands perfectly, what exactly happens next — the one action you want them to take?
questions.help: This is the CTA the whole arc builds toward. One concrete next step, not a list.
questions.interviewGuidance: Scope field. Sharpens the CTA + peak-end beat. Extensive mode asks it; quick mode may defer to the topic answer.

quick.note: SOP 9.1 simple set — 7 questions or fewer; one clarifying follow-up allowed only to close a CRITICAL gap (flag clarifying_followup:true).

in-depth.note: SOP 9.2 extensive set — 10 to 20 questions, follow-ups count toward the ceiling; a roughly-every-5-questions running summary keeps the owner oriented.

sessionBudget.on_overrun: When the budget is exceeded the driver forces a 'summarize and confirm' turn (read back what is captured, ask the owner to confirm or correct, then lock) instead of asking another open question.

completionContract.description: The intake is COMPLETE only when every mandatoryFieldOrder field is answered (asset_intake_question and pitch_included recorded verbatim) AND the owner has confirmed the read-back (interview_confirmed:true). deck-intake-driver.py --complete is the HARD precondition; it writes intake_ledger.json {complete:true, interview_confirmed:true} and, when present, feeds the existing scripts/qc-interview-completion.py. The deck build is DENIED (presentation-canonical-entry.sh GATE 0 + deck-build-guard.sh) until the ledger is complete.

oneVoiceRule: The client hears ONE seamless voice. The driver emits ONLY the next question (prompt + help). It NEVER emits routing/relay chatter: no 'relayed to the department', no 'the Director noted', no 'you'll see their next question shortly'. The department machinery is invisible to the client.
